title: Semantic HTML Rendering, Contextual Escaping, and Determinism · Language Clinic: Render Determinism
summary: Text nodes can inject markup, code is double-escaped, attributes are unquoted, heading IDs collide, dictionary order leaks into output, and raw HTML bypasses the trust model. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the render determinism increment in deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theor…
objectives: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.
Map headings, paragraphs, lists, quotes, code, links, images, tables, and landmarks to valid meaningful HTML with content constraints.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-render-determinism — Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-html-rendering-escaping-3
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-html-rendering-escaping-3.
# Case 5798: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def worked_static_site_html_rendering_escaping_3(nodes: int = 14, rendered_nodes: int = 14, escaped_values: int = 6, unsafe_contexts: int = 0, duplicate_ids: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "readerworked3",
        "source5798",
        "parse3dekrow",
        "routeworked35798",
        "artifact5798worked3",
        "recovery3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if nodes < 1 or rendered_nodes < 0:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-count"
    if rendered_nodes != nodes:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-coverage"
    if escaped_values < 1:
        return False, escaped_values, "escaping-evidence"
    if unsafe_contexts > 0 or duplicate_ids > 0:
        return False, unsafe_contexts + duplicate_ids, "unsafe-html"
    assert rendered_nodes == nodes
    return True, rendered_nodes, "semantic-safe-html"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Render Determinism evidence · Theory Render Determinism
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-render-determinism-1o6qen" that uses case 8327 to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Build Render Determinism evidence · Theory Render Determinism.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Render Determinism into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Build Render Determinism evidence · Theory Render Determinism, keep Render Determinism connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-render-determinism-1o6qen
# Competency: ssg-render-determinism.
# Case 8327: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG.
def evidence_ssg_render_determinism_1o6qen(nodes: int = 14, rendered_nodes: int = 14, escaped_values: int = 6, unsafe_contexts: int = 0, duplicate_ids: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1o6qen = "1o6qen-neq6o1-8327"
    assert evidence_variant_1o6qen
    evidence_axes_1o6qen = (
        "reader1o6qen",
        "source8327",
        "parseneq6o1",
        "route1o6qen8327",
        "artifact83271o6qen",
        "recoveryneq6o18327",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1o6qen)) == 6
    if nodes < 1 or rendered_nodes < 0:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-count"
    if rendered_nodes != nodes:
        return False, rendered_nodes, "render-coverage"
    if escaped_values < 1:
        return False, escaped_values, "escaping-evidence"
    if unsafe_contexts > 0 or duplicate_ids > 0:
        return False, unsafe_contexts + duplicate_ids, "unsafe-html"
    assert rendered_nodes == nodes
    return True, rendered_nodes, "semantic-safe-html"

content.text: The Render Determinism structural check is only one gate. deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Render Determinism model · Theory Render Determinism
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Render Determinism: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 8392 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to render every node as deterministic semantic HTML through context-safe boundaries; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Render Determinism model · Theory Render Determinism.
steps.why: Retrieving Render Determinism through the concrete deterministic context-safe semantic HTML renderer case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Render Determinism model · Theory Render Determinism, keep Render Determinism connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Stabilize ordering, whitespace policy, identifiers, serialization, and newline conventions and compare output digests across clean builds.
